title: Build shared atomic-write helper module
description: Foundational primitive — create packages/shared/src/node/atomic-write.ts exporting atomicWriteText, atomicWriteJson, createLedgerWriteQueue. Hand-rolled (no `atomically` npm dep). atomicWriteText uses tmp + rename pattern with optional fdatasync flag (default false, R3 signal handler will set true on shutdown). createLedgerWriteQueue returns path-keyed in-process Promise chain serializer. CLI re-exports retain backward compat by re-exporting from server/services/_shared.ts.
scope: Layer 0 infrastructure — atomic-write primitive consumed by all subsequent durability work

files.change: NEW: export atomicWriteText(path, content, { fsync? }), atomicWriteJson(path, value, { fsync?, indent? }), createLedgerWriteQueue() returning { append(path, line) } via path-keyed Promise chain. Tmp suffix = .<pid>.<ts>.<rand4>.tmp for crash-resume safety.
Add ./node/atomic-write to exports map alongside existing detector path
Re-export atomicWriteText from @fenglimg/fabric-shared/node/atomic-write to preserve backward compat for existing imports
NEW: unit tests covering happy path / fsync flag / queue serialization / queue rejection-recovery / tmp cleanup on rename success

criteria: atomicWriteText creates tmp file with .<pid>.<ts>.<rand4>.tmp suffix and atomically renames to target
atomicWriteText with fsync:true calls fdatasync before rename; default fsync:false skips it
createLedgerWriteQueue serializes appends per-path; rejection of one append does not poison subsequent appends to same path
atomicWriteJson is a thin wrapper with configurable indent (default 2)
All new tests pass via vitest in same commit
Existing pnpm test green (no regression in current atomicWriteText callers)
